name: Jundab ibn Amr
title: Martyr of the First Attack
shortDescription: A brave Shia who was present in Karbala with full awareness and was martyred in the valiant defense on the morning of Ashura.
fullStory:
Jundab ibn Amr is another hero and loyal man present in Karbala. With a heart full of love for the Prophet's (PBUH) family, he abandoned the world and its material goods to be by his Imam's side in the hardest conditions.

On the morning of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd shot the first arrow towards the Imam's tents and the Kufan army began a collective assault (known as the first attack), Jundab ibn Amr also drew his sword.

Shoulder to shoulder with the Imam's other loyal companions, they formed a solid front line of defense against the enemy. In this cowardly attack where arrows rained down, dozens of the Imam's companions were martyred.

Jundab, too, after a brave battle and repelling enemy attacks, attained the great honor of martyrdom in that very first attack, and his name was recorded among those near to the Divine Court.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Jundab ibn Amr.
